name: Mountain biking loop in the Serra d'Espadá
name_original: Circular en BTT por la Serra d'Espadá
description:
The Serra d'Espadà, with its lush cork oak forests and green valleys, is a corner of the Valencian Community where nature is shown in all its splendor, offering charming trails and panoramic views that invite tranquility and contemplation.

Eslida is the starting place of this adventure. In this beautiful town, full of narrow streets, public parking is available. Before starting the Route, make sure you bring supplies and, above all, water. The route challenges you at times with long and steep slopes. Also, if you decide to visit this area in summer, temperatures can be high. Therefore, we recommend avoiding the middle of the day or choosing any other time of year with more pleasant temperatures.

This circular route is made up of forest tracks, paths and paths that caress the mountainous terrain of the mountain range, offering you the best panoramic views from different angles of the route. In addition, you will find remains of past buildings full of history such as the Dalt mill.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Eslida offers a diverse landscape within the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, characterized by dramatic mountain ranges, lush forests of pine and cork oak, and traditional orchards. The terrain features significant ups and downs, with challenging climbs and descents across varied surfaces from sealed roads to technical mountain paths. This region is known for its distinctive red rock formations and numerous mountain passes, providing a rich environment for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Eslida The most popular mountain bike trail is Parque Natural de la Serra d'Espadá — Circular desde Chóvar por la Pista del Puntal de l'Aljub, a 31.7 miles (51.1 km) trail that takes 5 hours 26 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the natural park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Peñas Aragonesas and Serra d'Espadà Loop, a moderate 20.4 miles (32.8 km) path. This route offers varied terrain and scenic views of the surrounding mountains. Local mountain bikers also love the Sénia dels Nanos Ruins – Peñas Aragonesas loop from Artana, a 16.0 miles (25.8 km) trail leading through forested areas and past historical ruins, often completed in about 1 hour 45 minutes. Mountain biking around Eslida is defined by rugged and forested Sierra de Espadán, red rock formations, and challenging mountain passes. The extensive network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to difficult climbs. The routes in Eslida are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 130 reviews. More than 2500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Eslida's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Eslida?
A: Mountain biking around Eslida offers a diverse landscape within the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park. You'll encounter dramatic mountain ranges, lush forests of pine and cork oak, and traditional orchards. The terrain features significant ups and downs, with challenging climbs and descents across varied surfaces, from sealed roads to fire trails and more technical mountain paths. Look out for the distinctive red rock formations that characterize the area.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in the Eslida area?
A: There are over 190 mountain bike trails around Eslida listed on komoot, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Eslida?
A: Yes, Eslida offers a selection of easier routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31936687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sénia dels Nanos Ruins – Peñas Aragonesas loop from Artana&lt;/a&gt; is rated as easy and takes you through forested areas and past historical ruins.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for advanced riders?
A: Advanced riders will find plenty of challenging options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931286949/parque-natural-de-la-serra-d-espada-circular-desde-chovar-por-la-pista-del-puntal-de-l-aljub" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de la Serra d'Espadá — Circular desde Chóvar por la Pista del Puntal de l'Aljub&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 31.7-mile route with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1494768582/mountain-biking-loop-in-the-serra-d-espada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mountain biking loop in the Serra d'Espadá&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 36 miles with substantial climbing.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Eslida?
A: Yes, many routes in Eslida are circular. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931274960/penas-aragonesas-and-serra-d-espada-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas Aragonesas and Serra d'Espadà Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33833662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Espadà Valley – View of Benalí Castle loop from Eslida&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging circular ride with great views.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see while mountain biking near Eslida?
A: Eslida's trails often pass by fascinating historical sites. You can explore the remains of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2850704" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benalí Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the medieval &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2756864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1073196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Onda Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features the Molí de l'Aire (Air Mill) and the La Rambla Aqueduct, adding cultural interest to your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Eslida?
A: The Sierra de Espadán Natural Park offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are advisable, while winters are typically mild, making it possible to ride year-round, though some higher sections might be cooler.

Q: Are there good viewpoints along the mountain bike trails in Eslida?
A: Absolutely. The mountainous terrain of Eslida provides numerous panoramic viewpoints. Prominent peaks like the Puntal de l'Aljub offer breathtaking views of the Sierra de Espadán. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33833662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Espadà Valley – View of Benalí Castle loop from Eslida&lt;/a&gt;, are specifically designed to showcase the stunning vistas of the region.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Eslida?
A: The mountain bike trails around Eslida are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 130 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscape, the challenging climbs, and the rewarding views of the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park.

Q: Are there any mountain passes I can cycle through in Eslida?
A: Yes, Eslida is known for its mountain passes that are frequently part of cycling routes. You can ride through passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2472919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eslida Pass&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2477056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Nevera&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2972522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de la Vella&lt;/a&gt;, which offer challenging ascents and scenic descents.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike routes in Eslida?
A: While many routes in Eslida involve significant elevation changes and technical sections, there are easier trails that families with some biking experience might enjoy. Look for routes rated as 'easy' on komoot, which typically have less climbing and smoother surfaces, though specific family-focused routes are not explicitly highlighted in the data.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking trails in Eslida?
A: Eslida, being a small town within a natural park, generally offers parking options within the village itself or at designated trailheads. It's advisable to check specific route starting points on komoot for detailed parking information, as many routes begin directly from the town or nearby access points.
tips: From this point, you can enjoy a wide view of the majestic Benalí Castle, with the impressive surrounding peaks serving as a backdrop. The forest road that leads here is in perfect condition.
The attached pond is used both for irrigation and to drive the mill's water wheels.
It is a beautiful pass that winds towards Ain. There are also excellent views of Benalí Castle.
The fountain is located at the intersection of the road with the Agua Negra ravine. It is an extremely quiet place, surrounded by nature, perfect for taking a break and enjoying the surroundings.
It is an old building in ruins that preserves obvious signs of the passage of time. The surrounding environment is extremely pleasant and for many it could serve as a refuge in the middle of nature.

The road from Almedijar to the ruins of the Monte Rodana castle has sections of steep slope and has a mixture of concrete and dry land. The views are very beautiful as you ascend and leave the town behind.
